output.title: Landlord Withholding Your Security Deposit? Get It Back in 60 Seconds
output.meta_description: Is your landlord withholding your security deposit? Learn your tenant rights and generate a demand letter in 60 seconds for $19.99—no signup needed.
content_outline: How to Know If Your Landlord Is Illegally Withholding Your Security Deposit
What Landlords Can and Cannot Deduct: Know Your Tenant Rights
The Power of a Formal Security Deposit Dispute Letter
How to Demand Your Deposit Back in 60 Seconds Without a Lawyer
key_points: State laws require landlords to return security deposits within a specific timeframe, typically 14 to 60 days
Normal wear and tear—such as scuffed floors, faded paint, or minor nail holes—cannot be legally deducted
A professionally written demand letter is the fastest way to escalate before filing in small claims court
Deposits-Back.com generates state-specific deposit return demand letters in 60 seconds for a flat $19.99 fee with no signup or subscription
output.cta: Don't let your landlord keep what's yours. Generate your security deposit demand letter in 60 seconds at Deposits-Back.com for just $19.99—no signup, no subscription.
output.target_audience: Problem-aware US renters who are frustrated because their landlord is withholding, not returning, or wrongfully deducting from their security deposit

tiktok_reels.hook: Your landlord has 30 days. It's been 90.
tiktok_reels.script:
If your landlord is ghosting you about your security deposit, they're betting you'll just give up. Don't. Every state has a deadline—usually 14 to 30 days. After that? They're breaking the law.

You don't need a lawyer. You need a demand letter.

Deposits-Back.com writes one for you in 60 seconds. $19.99. No signup. Link in bio.

tiktok_reels.hook: They can't keep it for 'normal wear and tear' 👇
tiktok_reels.script:
Scuffed floors? Nail holes? Faded paint? That's normal wear and tear, and your landlord CANNOT deduct it. If they're withholding your deposit for fake damages, you have rights.

Fire back with a formal demand letter. Go to Deposits-Back.com, answer a few questions, and send a legally sound letter in under a minute. $19.99 flat. Get your money back.
